title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: jsplyr Shiny package moves data manipulation R server browser. implements dplyr’s lazy evaluation pattern (like dbplyr) instead generating SQL, generates JavaScript operations execute client-side JSON data. analogy: dbplyr databases jsplyr browser.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Interactive Shiny test apps (shinytest2) skipped non-interactive sessions. Run manually devtools::test(filter = "inst-apps") interactive R session.
code: # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file (regex matches after stripping test- prefix and .R extension) devtools::test(filter = "dots_to_query") devtools::test(filter = "tbl-lazy-json")  # Load package for interactive use pkgload::load_all()  # Generate docs (roxygen2 → man/, NAMESPACE) devtools::document()  # Full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Lint lintr::lint_package()

title: CLAUDE.md
code: R Server                          Browser (JavaScript) ────────                          ──────────────────── copy_to(session, df)  ──msg──►   jsonData[state_id] = [...]      │ filter/select/mutate  (lazy: just appends to compute_steps list)      │ compute()             ──msg──►   computeLazyJSON() executes steps sequentially      │                              │      │  ◄──HTTP POST──────────────  result JSON      │   promise resolves      │ collect()  →  tibble

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Lazy evaluation: dplyr verbs (filter, select, etc.) don’t execute — append compute_step() entries list tbl_lazy_json. Execution happens compute(). Promise-based collect: compute() creates one-shot HTTP endpoint via session$registerDataObj(), sends steps JS, returns promise. JS POSTs results back, resolving promise. collect() extracts value promise. Expression translation: R expressions parsed JavaScript strings. Column references get item. prefixed (e.g., age > 30 → item.age > 30). ifelse() calls translated ternary operators. State tracking: tbl_lazy_json state_id (random 30-char string) used key data browser’s global jsonData object.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tbl-lazy-json.R — Core tbl_lazy_json S3 class: holds session, state_id, compute_steps, .promise dots_to_query.R — Expression parsing: converts R expressions (quosures) JS-compatible strings filter/select/mutate/summarise verb-*.R — dplyr verb implementation appends compute step verb-compute.R — Sends accumulated steps JS via session$sendCustomMessage, registers HTTP callback verb-collect.R — Resolves promise compute, converts JSON tibble

title: CLAUDE.md
text: compute.js — Orchestrator: iterates compute steps, dispatches verb handlers, POSTs result back filter.js, select.js, distinct.js, mutate.js, group_by.js, summarise.js — Individual verb implementations copy_to.js — Stores data global jsonData object (R dataframe existing JS variable)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R dataframe → serialized JSON via jsonlite, sent browser via Shiny message Existing JS variable → referenced name (zero-copy), just registered jsonData

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Uses lintr 120-char line length, 45-char object names, inst/ excluded. See .lintr.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: testthat 3 edition snapshot tests tests/testthat/setup.R creates MockShinySession unit testing without browser Expression parsing (dots_to_query) lazy step accumulation tested R Full Shiny app integration tests via shinytest2 inst/test_app*/

what: Two ways to unwrap a promise
title: Working with collect() promises in reactive contexts
text: work context — reactive(), eventReactive(), observeEvent() observe() alike. Pick whichever reads better. examples use %...>% pipe.

previous_headings: Two ways to unwrap a promise
title: Working with collect() promises in reactive contexts
text: () registers callback runs data arrives. returns new promise, composes:
code: lazy_mtcars_query() |>   dplyr::collect() |>   promises::then(function(df) {     # `df` is the collected tibble     head(df)   })

previous_headings: Two ways to unwrap a promise
title: Working with collect() promises in reactive contexts
text: promises package ships “promise pipe”, %...>%, pipes resolved value next expression. reads just like regular pipe waits promise settle first. jsplyr re-exports , import promises : Use %...!% handle errors promise chain:
code: lazy_mtcars_query() |>   dplyr::collect() %...>%   head() lazy_mtcars_query() |>   dplyr::collect() %...>%   head() %...!%   (function(err) {     shiny::showNotification(conditionMessage(err), type = "error")   })

what: A practical example: updating an input from a computed value
title: Working with collect() promises in reactive contexts
text: common task set value input — say selectInput — number computed browser. Functions like shiny::updateSelectInput() side effects, reactive outputs. understand promises, pass collect()’s result straight selected — hand promise object instead value. Resolve promise first act value arrives. cleanest approach keeps updateSelectInput() call outside jsplyr pipeline. Store resolved value reactiveVal let separate observer update input. decouples “compute value” “update input”: %...>% pipe waits collected value arrive, . holds result tibble .$max_age written reactiveVal. second observer reacts change performs update. prefer keep computation reactive expression instead observer, return collected promise eventReactive() (gated button) resolve separate observe(): eventReactive() returns promise -, oldest_age() promise; observe() resolves %...>% updates input. plain reactive() works way want value recomputed whenever dependencies change rather button press. See inst/example_apps/app_update_select.R complete runnable app showing approaches.
code: # Holds the value computed in the browser. oldest_age <- shiny::reactiveVal(NULL)  # Pipeline: compute max(age) and store it. No UI update here. shiny::observeEvent(input$update, {   lazy_data() |>     dplyr::summarise("max_age = max(age)") |>     dplyr::collect() %...>% {     oldest_age(.$max_age)   } })  # Separate observer: update the input when the computed value changes. shiny::observeEvent(oldest_age(), {   shiny::updateSelectInput(     session,     inputId = "age",     selected = oldest_age()   ) }) # Gated on the button: returns the collect() promise. Computes only. oldest_age <- shiny::eventReactive(input$update, {   lazy_data() |>     dplyr::summarise("max_age = max(age)") |>     dplyr::collect() })  # Separate observer: resolve the promise and update the input. shiny::observe({   oldest_age() %...>% {     shiny::updateSelectInput(       session,       inputId = "age",       selected = .$max_age     )   } })

what: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
title: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
text: jsplyr JavaScript backend dplyr. Instead manipulating data Shiny server, pushes work browser, runs JSON data client-side. keeps data wrangling fast responsive even large data.frames, letting write familiar dplyr verbs already know. jsplyr still early stages development. check dplyr verbs supported check reference section.

title: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("jsplyr") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("r-world-devs/jsplyr")

title: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
text: First, UI need include_jsplyr() (sources Javascript code). Second, server part need call copy_to() register JSON data manipulation jsplyr. works similar dbplyr::copy_to() pass database connection input. difference create specific connection dbplyr, just make use shiny session (represents connection web browser). jsplyr takes account two cases: JSON data already defined browser JavaScript. case simply pass name used JavaScript copy_to(). data data.frame loaded server. case pass data.frame object function order send browser. Keep mind jsplyr works reactive context. copied JSON, can manipulate supported verbs. jsplyr, similar dbplyr, creates lazy representation data. Calling verbs simply registers next computation steps (like queries dbplyr) without triggering computation browser. retrieve data browser back server call collect(). triggers computation browser returns result. (also compute() step hood runs registered steps; need call — collect() runs .) find example application inst/example_apps folder.

title: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
text: Putting pieces together, minimal Shiny app copies data.frame browser, filters selects lazily based user input, renders collected result. data manipulation happens browser.
code: library(shiny) library(jsplyr)  ui <- fluidPage(   include_jsplyr(),   titlePanel("jsplyr example"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       numericInput("min_mpg", "Minimum mpg", value = 20),       selectInput(         "columns",         "Columns",         choices = names(mtcars),         selected = c("mpg", "cyl", "hp"),         multiple = TRUE       )     ),     mainPanel(       DT::DTOutput("table")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   lazy_mtcars <- reactive({     dplyr::copy_to(dest = session, df = mtcars)   })    output$table <- DT::renderDT({     lazy_mtcars() |>       dplyr::filter(mpg >= input$min_mpg) |>       dplyr::select(input$columns) |>       dplyr::collect()   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: R API to Manipulate JSON Data
text: collect() returns promise, result fetched asynchronously browser. Reactive outputs renderDT() resolve promises . reactive contexts — reactive(), eventReactive(), observeEvent() observe() — must handle promise (promises::() re-exported %...>% pipe). See vignette("collect--promises") details.

title: Add or modify columns in JSON data. — mutate
text: .data tbl_lazy_json object. ... Name-value pairs expressions. name gives name column output. value expression using existing columns, e.g. new_col = col1 + col2. Conditional helpers translated JavaScript: ifelse() if_else() become ternary operators, case_when() becomes chained set ternary operators. case_when() clauses without TRUE ~ ... catch-yield null unmatched rows, matching dplyr's NA default. across() expanded R side one column per selection. accepts single function (across(c(, b), round)), formula lambda (across(c(, b), ~ .x * 2)), named list functions (across(c(, b), list(double = ~ .x * 2))). Column selections accept c(...), bare column, character vector, all_of()/any_of(). Use .names {.col}/{.fn} glue placeholders control output column names; default single function reuses input name multiple functions produce {.col}_{.fn}.

title: Select rows of JSON data by position. — slice
text: .data tbl_lazy_json object. ... slice(), integer row positions keep (1-based). Negative positions drop rows. Unused variants. .preserve Ignored. Accepted consistency generic. n Number rows keep. Used slice_head(), slice_tail(), slice_min(), slice_max(). Defaults 1 applicable. prop Proportion rows keep (0-1), alternative n slice_head()/slice_tail()/slice_min()/slice_max(). , .Ignored. Accepted consistency generics; per-call grouping applied (use group_by(), slicing respects). order_by slice_min()/slice_max(), column order (bare name string). with_ties, na_rm Ignored. Accepted consistency slice_min()/slice_max() generics.

title: jsplyr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.

title: jsplyr 0.1.0
text: Added dplyr interface manipulating data JavaScript tbl(), copy_to(), collect(), compute(), show_query(). Added promise-based collect() decoupled shiny’s reactive input system. compute() uses session$registerDataObj() create session-scoped HTTP callback; JS side POSTs result , resolving promise. collect() require reactive context. Embedded compute() within collect() runs automatically called earlier pipeline. Re-exported promise pipe operators %...>%, %...!%, %...T>% promises, collect() results can consumed reactive contexts without attaching promises.

title: jsplyr 0.1.0
text: Added filter() verb subset rows predicate expressions, evaluated client-side. Comparisons (==, >, <, etc.) combined &/| supported. Values referenced input calling environment resolved R side; column references evaluated browser. Added select() verb choose columns name. Added distinct() verb keep unique rows. columns supplied ... determine uniqueness first row per unique combination kept; columns columns used. Supports .keep_all retain columns output. Added mutate() verb adding modifying columns using expressions. Supports non-standard evaluation character string inputs. Added ifelse() support mutate() expressions, translated JavaScript ternary operators. Nested ifelse() calls supported. Added if_else() alias ifelse() mutate() expressions; translate JavaScript ternary operators. Added case_when() support mutate() expressions, translated chained JavaScript ternary operators. Clauses without TRUE ~ ... catch-yield null unmatched rows, matching dplyr’s NA default. Added group_by() summarise() verbs support mean(), sum(), min(), max(), n(), sd(), median() aggregation functions. Added join verbs left_join(), right_join(), inner_join(), full_join(), semi_join(), anti_join(). Joining two tbl_lazy_json tables performed browser. Supports natural join (default), shared key, named vector differing key names. Colliding non-key columns get .x/.y suffixes. Added .na(), (), across()/if_all()/if_any() support filter() expressions. .na() () translated JavaScript browser side, across()/if_all()/if_any() expanded selected columns (combined & across()/if_all() | if_any()). Column selections accept c(...), bare column, character vector, all_of()/any_of(). Added across() support mutate() summarise(), all_of()/any_of()/across() column resolution group_by(). across() expanded R side selected columns accepts single function, formula lambda (~ .x * 2), named list functions, optional .names glue ({.col}/{.fn}). if_any()/if_all() remain filter()-. Added ungroup() remove grouping set group_by(). arguments grouping dropped; supplying column names removes columns grouping set (partial ungroup), matching dplyr::ungroup(). Added arrange() sort rows client-side one columns. Wrap column desc() descending order; multiple keys break ties left right sort stable. NA/null values sort last. Added rename() rename columns new = old pairs, preserving column order. Added slice() family: slice() (integer positions, negative positions drop rows), slice_head()/slice_tail() (first/last n prop), slice_min()/slice_max() (rows smallest/largest values column). Slicing group-aware data grouped group_by(). Added pull() extract single column vector. Like collect(), returns promises::promise() resolving vector. Supports selection name position, including negative indices counting last column. Added count() tally(). count() groups given columns counts rows per group; tally() counts within existing group_by(). honour name (default "n") sort arguments reuse group_by()/summarise() machinery. Added relocate() reorder columns using ./.placement, defaulting moving selected columns front. Added print() method tbl_lazy_json shows JSON source, lazy (-yet-computed) status, pending pipeline rendered dplyr-like calls. Mirrors dbplyr’s lazy tbl print, without retrieving data browser.

title: jsplyr 0.1.0
text: Added vignette("collect--promises") covering handle collect() promises inside reactive(), eventReactive(), observeEvent() observe(). Added example apps inst/example_apps/ showcasing jsplyr usage, including app_showcase.R app_update_select.R.

title: jsplyr 0.1.0
text: Added GitHub Actions workflows linting (lintr::lint_package()), standalone testthat run fast PR feedback, test coverage reporting via covr Codecov upload, version-bump check fails PRs bump DESCRIPTION Version. Added pkgdown workflow builds documentation site deploys GitHub Pages. Added R-CMD-check, Lint, Codecov, CRAN downloads badges README.
